title: Fixing CSS Styling for Responsive Web Design

statement.markdown: A web developer has created a simple responsive website with a CSS file that applies different styles based on screen width. However, there's a bug in the CSS that prevents the website from rendering correctly on smaller screens. Fix the bug by identifying and correcting the logical error in the CSS code.
statement.inputFormat: A string representing the CSS code to be fixed
statement.notes: The website should render correctly on all screen sizes, including small screens.

constraints.description: No specific constraints for this problem

examples.explanation: No changes are needed for this example. The CSS code is already correct.
The bug is that the width is not decreasing correctly for smaller screens.

hints.text: Check the media queries for correct usage of screen width

editorial.title: Fixing CSS Media Queries
editorial.intuition: Identify the logical error in the CSS code and correct it by adding the missing media query
editorial.bruteForce: Manually check each media query and adjust the width accordingly
editorial.betterSolution: Use a more efficient approach by using a single media query with a max-width condition
editorial.optimalSolution: The corrected CSS code should have a single media query with a max-width condition that decreases the width correctly for smaller screens
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The website should render correctly on all screen sizes, including small screens
